perturbation.original_text: 1.15 "Professional Component" shall mean the performance of the professional component of the steps of the Assay, which is the interpretation of results (generated in the Technical Component) of an Assay by a pathologist, and is covered by CPT codes from the Professional Fee Schedule with the modifier "26".
perturbation.changed_text: 1.15 "Professional Component" shall mean the performance of the professional component of the steps of the Assay, which may include, but is not limited to, the interpretation of results (generated in the Technical Component) of an Assay by a pathologist, or a qualified technician, and is covered by CPT codes from the Professional Fee Schedule with the modifier "26", or any other applicable modifier.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'or a qualified technician' and 'or any other applicable modifier,' the definition of 'Professional Component' becomes ambiguous. Previously, it was clearly defined as interpretation by a pathologist using CPT codes with modifier '26'. Now, it's unclear who is qualified to perform the Professional Component. This change contradicts the sections of the contract that relies on that 'Professional Component' to be performed only by a pathologist, and creates uncertainty about payment obligations tied to specific CPT codes and modifiers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Test Performance. Life Technologies shall have the responsibility, subject to its capacity to support in its reasonable discretion (of which capacity Life Technologies shall notify Biocept in writing at least sixty (60) days before launch of the Assay, and use diligent efforts to notify Biocept at least thirty (30) days before discovery of any decreases or increases in such capacity), for performing the Professional Component of the Assays sold by either party in the Territory. In particular, the laboratory director of the Life Technologies CLIA laboratory will be responsible for issuing and signing off on the report.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity into the definition of 'Professional Component' by allowing a 'qualified technician' to perform it, whereas the contradicted text explicitly states that 'the laboratory director of the Life Technologies CLIA laboratory will be responsible for issuing and signing off on the report,' implying a pathologist and not a technician. This contradicts the definition of that term creating an ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: 8.3 New Technology. In the course of the activities conducted by the parties, Biocept and/or Life Technologies may conceive of inventions or discoveries or create works that constitute intellectual property and may be patentable or registerable as a copyright or other intellectual property right (all of the foregoing, including such intellectual property rights therein, collectively, "Developments"). Inventorship of all inventions and discoveries, whether or not patentable, will be determined in accordance with United States patent laws. Authorship of all copyrightable works will be determined in accordance with United States copyright laws. Subject to Section 8.2, as between the parties, Developments will be owned consistent with such determination of inventorship or authorship. To the extent any Development owned by Life Technologies relates directly to the practice of, or constitutes an improvement to, the Assay, Life Technologies hereby grants to Biocept, during the Term of this Agreement, and, except in the case of termination of this Agreement by Life Technologies for Biocept's uncured material breach, after expiration or termination of this Agreement, a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free, fully-paid license, including the right to sublicense, under Life Technologies' Intellectual Property Rights in such Developments, solely to develop, make, have made, use, sell, have sold, offer for sale, import, perform and provide the Assay. To the extent any Development owned by Biocept relates directly to the practice of, or constitutes an improvement to, the Assay, Biocept hereby grants to Life Technologies, during the Term of this Agreement, a non-exclusive license under Biocept's Intellectual Property Rights in such Development, solely to promote the Assay in the Territory and to perform the Professional Component of the Assay sold by the parties in the Territory, in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.3 New Technology. In the course of the activities conducted by the parties, Biocept and/or Life Technologies may conceive of inventions or discoveries or create works that constitute intellectual property and may be patentable or registerable as a copyright or other intellectual property right (all of the foregoing, including such intellectual property rights therein, collectively, "Developments"). Inventorship of all inventions and discoveries, whether or not patentable, will be determined in accordance with United States patent laws, unless otherwise agreed upon. Authorship of all copyrightable works will be determined in accordance with United States copyright laws, unless business ethics determines otherwise. Subject to Section 8.2, as between the parties, Developments will be owned consistent with such determination of inventorship or authorship. To the extent any Development owned by Life Technologies relates directly to the practice of, or constitutes an improvement to, the Assay, Life Technologies hereby grants to Biocept, during the Term of this Agreement, and, except in the case of termination of this Agreement by Life Technologies for Biocept's uncured material breach, after expiration or termination of this Agreement, a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free, fully-paid license, including the right to sublicense, under Life Technologies' Intellectual Property Rights in such Developments, solely to develop, make, have made, use, sell, have sold, offer for sale, import, perform and provide the Assay. To the extent any Development owned by Biocept relates directly to the practice of, or constitutes an improvement to, the Assay, Biocept hereby grants to Life Technologies, during the Term of this Agreement, a non-exclusive license under Biocept's Intellectual Property Rights in such Development, solely to promote the Assay in the Territory and to perform the Professional Component of the Assay sold by the parties in the Territory, in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The phrases 'unless otherwise agreed upon' and 'unless business ethics determines otherwise' introduces a contradiction, because the original section specifies inventorship will be according to US patent laws, now it is introducing the possibility of other factors that may influence inventorship. This will influence the decision of ownership as well. This contradicts the intent of the original term, rendering the ownership of new technologies and intellectual property created from the collaboration uncertain, which could lead to disagreements about commercial rights, licensing, and patent enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.3 New Technology. In the course of the activities conducted by the parties, Biocept and/or Life Technologies may conceive of inventions or discoveries or create works that constitute intellectual property and may be patentable or registerable as a copyright or other intellectual property right (all of the foregoing, including such intellectual property rights therein, collectively, "Developments"). Inventorship of all inventions and discoveries, whether or not patentable, will be determined in accordance with United States patent laws, unless otherwise agreed upon. Authorship of all copyrightable works will be determined in accordance with United States copyright laws, unless business ethics determines otherwise. Subject to Section 8.2, as between the parties, Developments will be owned consistent with such determination of inventorship or authorship. To the extent any Development owned by Life Technologies relates directly to the practice of, or constitutes an improvement to, the Assay, Life Technologies hereby grants to Biocept, during the Term of this Agreement, and, except in the case of termination of this Agreement by Life Technologies for Biocept's uncured material breach, after expiration or termination of this Agreement, a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free, fully-paid license, including the right to sublicense, under Life Technologies' Intellectual Property Rights in such Developments, solely to develop, make, have made, use, sell, have sold, offer for sale, import, perform and provide the Assay. To the extent any Development owned by Biocept relates directly to the practice of, or constitutes an improvement to, the Assay, Biocept hereby grants to Life Technologies, during the Term of this Agreement, a non-exclusive license under Biocept's Intellectual Property Rights in such Development, solely to promote the Assay in the Territory and to perform the Professional Component of the Assay sold by the parties in the Territory, in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty by adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon' and 'unless business ethics determines otherwise' to the determination of inventorship and authorship, which was originally stated to be determined solely by United States patent and copyright laws. This contradicts the original intent of a clear, legally defined process for determining ownership of intellectual property, potentially leading to disputes and rendering the original clause less enforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' where terms are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.
